commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=29226cbae7fe825cca28b23defef0568e879f389)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds opt-in userspace Tailscale egress for Islo leases, claim and pond metadata handling, proxy injection, lifecycle recovery and validation, docs, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. I reviewed the diff and current main behavior; the current PR text claims live validation, but I do not see inspectable current-head runtime output in the visible body/comments.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor-provided current-head proof and maintainer rollout judgment; the changelog cleanup is mechanical but not enough for an automated repair lane while proof is missing.

target.title: feat(islo): add Tailscale plane to the islo provider
target.body:
## Summary

Adds opt-in userspace Tailscale egress to Islo leases through the existing Islo exec stream.

Islo remains URL-primary for dialable pond traffic. `FeatureTailscale` means outbound proxy access only: Crabbox does not advertise the sandbox's `100.x` address as an inbound pond endpoint.

## Behavior

When a new Islo lease is created with `--tailscale`, Crabbox:

1. downloads Tailscale 1.98.4 and verifies the architecture-specific SHA-256 digest;
2. starts `tailscaled` with memory-only node state, `--tun=userspace-networking`, and `--shields-up=true`;
3. binds SOCKS5 and HTTP proxy listeners on `127.0.0.2:1055`;
4. passes the auth key through stdin rather than argv or a filesystem file;
5. records enrollment and recovery settings on the lease claim;
6. injects upper- and lowercase proxy defaults into enrolled workload commands;
7. keeps the URL bridge as the primary `pond peers` transport and reports Tailscale as outbound-only.

Explicit workload proxy environment values override the defaults. An explicit `ALL_PROXY` or `all_proxy` suppresses the protocol-specific defaults.

Enrollment is creation-time only. A reused plain lease cannot be enrolled in place. Reused enrolled leases revalidate or repair the daemon before execution and fail closed rather than silently using direct egress when the proxy path is unavailable.

## Security and lifecycle

- Node identity uses `--state=mem:` so filesystem snapshots cannot clone a Tailscale private key.
- The auth key is held only in memory and sent to `tailscale up` through stdin.
- The supplied direct auth key must be reusable and ephemeral for recovery without persistent stale device records.
- Tailscale binaries, socket, PID, logs, and recovery lock live in a root-only per-lease directory.
- Crabbox tracks and terminates only the daemon associated with the lease's exact control socket before replacement.
- Repository sync and workloads run as Islo's non-root `islo` user.
- Workspace ownership is repaired on every enrolled run and after native archive uploads.
- Paused enrolled sandboxes resume before repair; read-only status and pond discovery use bounded health checks without starting the long repair path.
- Failed creation-time enrollment rolls back the new sandbox and local claim. Kept leases retain session metadata on later preparation failures.

## Config and secrets

- `CRABBOX_TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY`: reusable, ephemeral direct-provider node auth key.
- `TS_CONTROL_URL`: optional control-plane URL, passed as `--login-server`.
- `--tailscale-tags`: advertised tags.
- `--tailscale-exit-node` and `--tailscale-exit-node-allow-lan-access`: apply to traffic using the userspace proxy.

No Islo service or SDK changes are required.

## Verification

Final local gate:

```sh
go test -race ./...
go vet ./...
go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.45.0 -test ./...
scripts/check-docs.sh
npm run format:check --prefix worker
npm run lint --prefix worker
npm run check --prefix worker
npm test --prefix worker
npm run build --prefix worker
```

Worker result: 14 files and 349 tests passed; Wrangler dry-run build succeeded.

Structured review:

```sh
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --stream-engine-output
```

Final result: no accepted/actionable findings.

Live Islo validation on June 4, 2026 proved creation-time userspace enrollment, assigned tailnet addresses, and proxy-routed traffic between enrolled sandboxes. The final reviewed contract intentionally exposes that capability as outbound-only and keeps URL shares as the dialable pond transport.

## Release note

Adds an opt-in Islo userspace Tailscale egress path with URL-primary pond discovery. Thanks @zozo123.
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
